pi_items.source: task.json description (line 5) and export_result.sh (line 15) both specify this exact path

Physically reasonable: 1 mm is a conservative lower bound. Research shows HAWT out-of-plane blade deflections range from 0 to 21.66 m (https://cdn.aui.ma/sse-capstone-repository/pdf/spring2021/Small%20Wind%20Turbine%20Blade%20Tip%20Deflection%20.pdf). 0.001 m is well below typical operating deflections.

Aligns with research data showing typical out-of-plane blade-tip deflections reaching 21.66 m for HAWT turbines. Research paper documents maximum out-of-plane deflection of 3.78 m for certain turbine designs (https://cdn.aui.ma/sse-capstone-repository/pdf/spring2021/Small%20Wind%20Turbine%20Blade%20Tip%20Deflection%20.pdf). The max of 20.0 m is within published range.

Verifier code comment indicates '1.0' is a sanity check threshold (verifier.py line 79: 'Moment can be huge, just check > 1'). Could not find published typical values for HAWT_Test.wpa sample project. Root moments scale with R^4, so small test turbines would have correspondingly small moments. 1.0 Nm appears to be validation threshold rather than typical operating value.

Verified: Multiple sources confirm 12 m/s is the typical wind speed at or near rated power output for HAWT turbines (https://electricalacademia.com/renewable-energy/horizontal-axis-wind-turbine-hawt-working-principle-single-blade-two-blade-three-blade-wind-turbine/). Task operates in the transition/rated power region.

Task involves running QBlade simulations to generate aerodynamic loads (BEM simulation at 12 m/s) and structural deflection analysis. HAWT_Test.wpa is a sample project provided with QBlade. Data is computed, not measured from real wind turbine.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses QBlade software to assess synthetic blade deflection data from a HAWT sample project. The report path (/home/ga/Documents/deflection_report.txt) is correctly specified. Deflection bounds (0.001-20.0 m) are physically reasonable and align with literature on HAWT blade deflections. The target wind speed of 12 m/s corresponds to typical HAWT rated-power operating conditions. The minimum moment threshold of 1.0 Nm functions as a validation check. All synthetic data is generated through QBlade's BEM aerodynamic and structural analysis modules.
